expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 3 synthesis template includes one section per persona (e.g. '### Henk Bakker (Elderly)', '### Fatima (Language barrier)') summarising each agent's individual findings before the cross-persona aggregation.
SKILL.md Step 3 synthesis template has a '## Cross-Persona Patterns' section listing issues confirmed by 2+ personas with the agreement count and which personas were affected.

expectations.text: Report uses structured COUNSEL_TEST_RESULT output line
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 4 specifies the exact output format: 'COUNSEL_TEST_RESULT: PASS | FAIL  CRITICAL_COUNT: <n>  SUMMARY: <one-line>'. This structured line is machine-parseable for the pipeline and would not be produced by naive Claude.

expectations.text: Offers to create OpenSpec changes for discovered gaps
expectations.evidence: SKILL.md Step 4 explicitly: 'If gaps identified → offer to create OpenSpec changes for them.' This connects test findings back into the OpenSpec workflow — a Conduction-specific integration absent from general-purpose testing.

notes: All four report format requirements are explicitly specified in SKILL.md Steps 3–4. The COUNSEL_TEST_RESULT structured output line and OpenSpec gap-creation offer are the most uniquely skill-specific elements.
